channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇪🇺 European countries emerge as NATO leaders as U.S. role recedes
item.description: President Trump decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions, pushing European leaders to consider a future where the U.S. no longer leads the alliance. Source: NPR | Category: Europe

item.title: 🚢 Spain readies evacuations as hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
item.description: Spanish authorities prepare to receive over 140 passengers and crew from a cruise ship where at least three passengers have died. US and UK arranging repatriation flights for their citizens. Source: AP News | Category: Europe

item.title: 🌋 Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on remote island
item.description: Mount Dukono erupted on Halmahera Island, killing three hikers including two Singaporeans. Rescuers evacuated 14 climbers while searching for others who defied safety restrictions. Source: AP News | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇨🇩 Islamic State-affiliated group kills 40 in Congo near Uganda border
item.description: Allied Democratic Forces attacked villages in eastern Congo overnight, killing at least 40 and burning homes. The group pledged allegiance to IS in 2019 and has long operated in the border region. Source: AP News | Category: Africa

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon civil defense member killed in Israeli strike amid ceasefire
item.description: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a civil defense rescuer despite the ongoing truce. WHO has verified 152 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon since the war began, with 103 deaths. Source: CBS News | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, according to Chinese media, as the US launched and then suspended a plan to help stranded vessels transiting the vital waterway. Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: ⚔️ US carries out retaliatory strikes on Iran after Hormuz clash
item.description: The US military conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets it said were responsible for attacking American forces, calling the hostilities "unprovoked" amid renewed fighting in the Gulf region. Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇨🇳 China sees weakened US deterrence on Taiwan as Iran war drains weapons
item.description: Chinese analysts say America"s ability to deter a war over Taiwan is weakened by the Iran conflict, giving Beijing leverage ahead of President Trump"s upcoming summit with Xi Jinping. Source: New York Times | Category: US-China

item.title: 🇦🇪🇵🇸 UAE sends $100 million to train new Gaza police force under Trump-backed plan
item.description: The UAE has transferred $100 million to Trump's Board of Peace to fund training of a 27,000-strong Gaza police force composed of Jordanian and Egyptian-trained officers, marking a significant step in post-war governance planning. Source: Middle East Eye | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇰🇮🇳 China's J-35 stealth fighters to give Pakistan edge over India in fifth-generation tech
item.description: Pakistan is set to acquire Chinese J-35 stealth jets, potentially giving it a technological advantage over India which lacks fifth-generation fighters despite having a larger overall fleet. Source: SCMP | Category: South Asia

item.title: 🇩🇪💻 German cyber chief warns: 'We are not at war, but we're not at peace either'
item.description: Germany's cyber warfare commander says allied nations face relentless digital attacks from Russia and other adversaries, with cyber intrusions increasingly targeting defense industries as European rearmament accelerates. Source: DW | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇭🎯 US-Philippine Balikatan drills expand with more nations and advanced missiles
item.description: American and Filipino forces wrapped up annual exercises featuring troops from five additional nations and deployed advanced systems including NMESIS and HIMARS, demonstrating deepening Indo-Pacific security cooperation. Source: Stripes | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇺🇦🇸🇪 Ukraine-Sweden Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
item.description: Ukraine's defense minister says talks for Saab-made Gripen fighter jets are progressing well, with a potential deal signed within months during his visit to Stockholm. Source: Reuters | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇰🇵⚔️ North Korea Deploys New Artillery Systems Capable of Striking Seoul
item.description: Kim Jong Un inspected new long-range artillery with ranges exceeding 60km to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering ability to hit Seoul, state media reports. Source: Reuters | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 💰🛢️ $7 Billion in Well-Timed Oil Bets Before Iran War News Sparks Scrutiny
item.description: Legal experts and lawmakers are calling for investigations into whether well-timed oil trades before the Iran war news were based on inside information or leaks. Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🏅🇧🇾 IOC Lifts All Restrictions on Belarusian Athletes
item.description: The International Olympic Committee removed all restrictions on Belarusian athletes, clearing them for international competitions including LA 2028 Olympic qualifiers. Source: Reuters | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Vance and Rubio Take Center Stage as 2028 Republican Contenders
item.description: The Vice President and Secretary of State offered contrasting styles this week, signaling early positioning for a potential post-Trump Republican future. Source: Reuters | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ UK Labour Suffers Heavy Losses as Reform UK Makes Gains
item.description: Partial results from English local elections show large losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party and gains for the hard-right Reform UK party. Source: AP News | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇬🇧🇨🇳 British MPs to Visit China for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of UK lawmakers will visit China this month for the first time since 2019, signaling warming ties following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit. Source: Reuters | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪⚔️ Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions with NATO allies widen over the Iran war, raising questions about the future of the transatlantic alliance. Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇨🇳💰 China Export Growth Quickens on Iran War Stockpiling
item.description: China's export growth likely accelerated in April as companies rushed to stockpile components amid fears the Iran war could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows. Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🌏⚖️ ASEAN Leaders Meet on Iran War Economic Fallout
item.description: Southeast Asian leaders are meeting under pressure to mitigate the impact on their economies from the Iran war, with one minister saying it "should not have occurred in the first place." Source: AP News | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇯🇵 Toyota Forecasts 20% Profit Drop as Iran War Disrupts Supply Chains
item.description: Toyota halved its quarterly profit and forecast a 20% annual decline as supply chain disruptions from the Iran war and rising costs weigh on the world's largest automaker. Source: Channel News Asia | Category: Economy

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Seeks Singapore's Help for Return of Seafarers From US-Seized Vessels
item.description: Pakistan's foreign minister requested Singapore's assistance in repatriating Pakistani and Iranian seafarers aboard vessels seized by the US amid the Iran conflict. Source: Anadolu Agency | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Opposition Bloc Could Secure 61 Seats Under Three-Party Merger, Poll Shows
item.description: A new poll suggests a merger between Bennett, Eisenkot, and Liberman's parties could form a 61-seat opposition bloc without relying on Arab parties, challenging Netanyahu's coalition. Source: Jerusalem Post | Category: Middle East
